title: Design Reasoning Protocol Layer (RPL) Architecture
description: Define the structure, purpose, and integration points for the Reasoning Protocol Layer.
details: Create a high-level architecture document describing how the RPL fits between the model, CPU/state machine, and skill arbitration layers. Specify the reasoning schema, message envelope extensions, and how reasoning traces are logged or suppressed. Include diagrams showing data flow and lifecycle.
testStrategy: Verify the architecture document is complete, internally consistent, and covers all integration points. Review with future tasks to ensure feasibility.

subtasks.title: Define RPL goals and constraints
Map RPL to CPU/state-machine lifecycle
Define reasoning schema and envelope fields

title: Implement Reasoning Schema
description: Add the structured reasoning schema used internally by the RPL.
details: Implement a structured reasoning schema with fields such as: goal, context, tool_evaluation, memory_considerations, plan, and uncertainty. Ensure schema is serializable, versioned, and compatible with existing message envelopes. Add Rust types and validation.
testStrategy: Unit test schema serialization/deserialization. Validate schema against example reasoning traces. Confirm compatibility with existing message structures.

subtasks.title: Create Rust types for reasoning schema
Implement schema validation
Add versioning and compatibility layer

title: Integrate RPL with CPU/State Machine
description: Connect the Reasoning Protocol Layer to the CPU execution lifecycle.
details: Modify the CPU/state machine to call into the RPL at key lifecycle stages: pre-evaluation, tool selection, memory lookup, and final action planning. Ensure deterministic behavior and clean separation of concerns. Add hooks for reasoning injection and extraction.
testStrategy: Run CPU lifecycle tests ensuring reasoning steps are invoked at correct times. Validate that reasoning does not break existing tool execution or message flow.

subtasks.title: Add RPL hooks to CPU lifecycle
Implement reasoning injection/extraction
Ensure deterministic execution ordering

title: Integrate RPL with Skill Arbitration
description: Use the RPL to influence skill scoring, ranking, and fallback logic.
details: Modify the skill arbitration layer to consume reasoning outputs such as tool_evaluation and uncertainty. Allow RPL to provide weighted recommendations. Ensure arbitration remains deterministic and overrideable.
testStrategy: Simulate multiple arbitration scenarios and confirm RPL influences scoring correctly. Validate fallback behavior when reasoning is incomplete or suppressed.

subtasks.title: Connect RPL outputs to skill scoring
Implement uncertainty-aware fallback logic

title: Add Reasoning Trace Logging
description: Implement structured logging for reasoning traces.
details: Add structured logs for reasoning steps using correlation IDs and the unified logging system. Ensure logs can be toggled, redacted, or suppressed. Add log levels for reasoning: trace, debug, summary.
testStrategy: Verify logs appear correctly in trace mode and are suppressed in normal mode. Confirm correlation IDs match CPU execution logs.

subtasks.title: Implement reasoning log format
Add log-level controls for reasoning

title: Add Reasoning Suppression and Privacy Controls
description: Ensure reasoning traces never leak unless explicitly requested.
details: Implement a suppression layer that prevents reasoning traces from being returned to the user unless a debug flag is set. Add redaction rules for sensitive memory or internal state. Ensure compliance with safety and privacy constraints.
testStrategy: Test that reasoning traces never appear in normal responses. Validate that debug mode shows traces only when explicitly enabled.

subtasks.title: Implement suppression layer
Add redaction rules

title: End-to-End RPL Integration Test Suite
description: Create a full test suite validating the entire Reasoning Protocol Layer.
details: Build integration tests covering: schema correctness, CPU lifecycle integration, skill arbitration influence, logging, suppression, and deterministic behavior. Include regression tests to prevent reasoning leakage.
testStrategy: Run full suite and confirm all reasoning behaviors match expected outputs. Validate no regressions in existing Grok-CLI functionality.

subtasks.title: Create integration test harness
Write full RPL test scenarios

title: Design Full Reasoning Engine Integrated with CPU
description: Define the architecture for a full reasoning engine tightly integrated into the CPU/state machine.
details: Design a reasoning engine that operates as a first-class component in the CPU lifecycle. It should orchestrate goal analysis, planning, tool selection, memory usage, uncertainty handling, and self-correction. Define clear interfaces between the CPU, Reasoning Engine, MemoryManager, and Skill Arbitration. Specify how reasoning states are represented, advanced, and persisted across turns.
testStrategy: Review the architecture document for completeness and consistency. Validate that all CPU phases have corresponding reasoning phases and that interfaces are well-defined and testable.

subtasks.title: Define reasoning engine responsibilities and boundaries
Map CPU phases to reasoning phases
Specify interfaces to MemoryManager and Skill Arbitration

title: Implement Reasoning Engine Core State Model
description: Create the core state structures and transitions for the reasoning engine.
details: Implement Rust types representing the reasoning engine state, including fields for current goal, hypotheses, candidate plans, selected tools, memory references, and uncertainty metrics. Define explicit state transitions (e.g., AnalyzeGoal, ExpandOptions, EvaluateOptions, CommitPlan, ExecuteStep, RevisePlan). Ensure the state model is serializable and compatible with existing message envelopes and RPL schema.
testStrategy: Add unit tests for state transitions, ensuring valid transitions succeed and invalid transitions are rejected. Verify serialization/deserialization round-trips without data loss.

subtasks.title: Define reasoning state enums and structs
Implement state transition functions
Add serialization and versioning for reasoning state

title: Integrate Bayesian Reasoning into Reasoning Engine
description: Wire Bayesian belief updates into the reasoning engine’s decision process.
details: Connect the existing Bayesian reasoning layer to the reasoning engine so that hypotheses, tool choices, and plans are scored and updated using probabilistic beliefs. Define how evidence from tool results, model outputs, and memory lookups updates beliefs. Ensure the Bayesian layer can influence plan selection, fallback decisions, and self-correction triggers.
testStrategy: Create tests where controlled evidence updates beliefs in predictable ways. Verify that belief changes affect plan selection and fallback behavior as expected.

subtasks.title: Define belief structures for hypotheses and tools
Implement evidence update hooks in reasoning engine
Connect Bayesian scores to plan and tool selection

title: Add Planning and Multi-Step Execution Logic
description: Enable the reasoning engine to construct and manage multi-step plans executed via the CPU.
details: Implement a planning layer inside the reasoning engine that can decompose goals into ordered steps, each mapped to tools, memory operations, or model calls. Integrate with the CPU/state machine so that each plan step corresponds to a CPU action. Support plan revision when evidence contradicts assumptions or when steps fail.
testStrategy: Create scenarios where a goal requires multiple steps and verify that the engine builds a plan, executes steps in order, and revises the plan when intermediate results differ from expectations.

subtasks.title: Implement plan representation and step types
Connect plan steps to CPU actions
Implement plan revision and re-planning logic

title: Integrate Reasoning Engine with MemoryManager
description: Make the reasoning engine memory-aware for context, retrieval, and write-back decisions.
details: Add hooks for the reasoning engine to request memory lookups, evaluate which memories are relevant, and decide when to write new memories. Use reasoning state (goal, hypotheses, uncertainty) to guide which memories to fetch. Ensure memory operations are explicit steps in the plan and are logged via the RPL.
testStrategy: Test scenarios where memory is required to answer a query or continue a multi-turn interaction. Verify that the reasoning engine requests appropriate memory lookups and writes back useful summaries or state.

subtasks.title: Define memory interaction API for reasoning engine
Implement memory-aware reasoning steps
Add tests for memory-driven reasoning decisions

title: Deep Integration with Skill Arbitration and Tool Planning
description: Use the reasoning engine to drive tool selection, ordering, and fallback strategies.
details: Refactor skill arbitration so that it becomes a collaborative process with the reasoning engine. The engine should propose candidate tools and sequences based on the plan, while arbitration scores and constraints refine the final selection. Support uncertainty-aware fallbacks, parallelizable tool steps, and explicit no-op decisions when tools are not needed.
testStrategy: Simulate multiple tool-heavy scenarios and verify that the reasoning engine and arbitration jointly select appropriate tools, handle failures gracefully, and avoid unnecessary calls.

subtasks.title: Expose tool capabilities and costs to reasoning engine
Implement joint reasoning–arbitration selection flow
Add uncertainty-aware fallback and retry logic

title: Self-Correction and Error Recovery Loops
description: Enable the reasoning engine to detect mistakes and trigger self-correction cycles.
details: Implement mechanisms for the reasoning engine to detect inconsistencies, low-confidence outcomes, or user feedback indicating failure. When triggered, the engine should re-evaluate beliefs, revise plans, or choose alternative tools. Ensure loops are bounded to avoid infinite retries and are fully observable in reasoning traces and logs.
testStrategy: Create tests where initial plans are flawed or tools fail. Verify that the engine detects issues, revises its approach, and converges on a better outcome or gracefully reports limitations.

subtasks.title: Define error signals and self-correction triggers
Implement self-correction loop logic
Add safeguards against infinite correction loops

title: Reasoning Engine Observability and Debug Controls
description: Expose rich but safe observability for the reasoning engine integrated with existing logging and RPL.
details: Extend the existing RPL logging to capture reasoning engine states, transitions, and decisions with correlation IDs. Add debug flags or modes to enable deep reasoning traces for development while keeping them suppressed or summarized in normal operation. Ensure logs are structured, filterable, and respect privacy and redaction rules.
testStrategy: Run end-to-end flows in both normal and debug modes. Verify that debug mode shows detailed reasoning traces, while normal mode only shows high-level summaries or none at all.

subtasks.title: Connect reasoning engine state to structured logs
Implement debug flags and modes for reasoning
Validate redaction and privacy behavior in logs

title: End-to-End Reasoning Engine CPU Integration Tests
description: Build a comprehensive test suite for the fully integrated reasoning engine and CPU.
details: Create an integration test suite that exercises the full reasoning engine lifecycle: goal analysis, planning, Bayesian updates, memory usage, tool selection, self-correction, and final response generation. Include regression tests to ensure existing Grok-CLI behaviors remain stable and that reasoning does not leak unintentionally.
testStrategy: Run the full suite on representative scenarios (simple Q&A, multi-step tasks, tool-heavy flows, memory-dependent conversations). Confirm that outputs are correct, deterministic where expected, and that reasoning behavior matches the designed architecture.

subtasks.title: Create integration test harness for CPU + reasoning engine
Author scenario-based tests covering all reasoning phases
Add regression tests for existing Grok-CLI behaviors
